subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, narrowly focused and testable, with key variables and measurement criteria defined unambiguously.
The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with comprehensive scientific background and engaging explanations of plant chemistry and bacterial mechanisms.
Methodology is well justified: variables are selected with clear rationale, full procedures are detailed, and appropriate statistical tests are chosen.
Throughout the report, sections consistently link back to the research question, demonstrating exceptional focus and coherence.
weaknesses: Justification for the chosen sample size (replication number) is not explicitly provided.
Occasional digressions (e.g., cultural background of yerba-mate) momentarily detract from the scientific focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the student in the top band for topic communication and research question formulation (clear, focused, testable) and close to top for methodology. Although there are minor methodological lapses (sample-size rationale) and small digressions, the overall focus, clarity and coherence throughout justify awarding the maximum 6 marks.

strengths: Frequent and mostly relevant integration of literature on yerba-mate chemistry and antibacterial studies to justify hypotheses and interpret results.
Accurate and consistent use of scientific terminology (e.g., zone of inhibition, MIC, ANOVA) throughout the report.
weaknesses: Reliance on generic or non-peer-reviewed sources (e.g., Wikipedia) undermines source reliability and depth of application.
Background sections occasionally become descriptive (listing compounds without critical selection), limiting analytical use of source material.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates mostly relevant application of source material and strong command of terminology, fitting the 3â4 band. However, limited critical comparison of differing findings and reliance on generic sources prevent reaching the 5â6 band, so 4 marks appropriately reflect this performance.

strengths: Data are processed correctly (means, standard deviations) and inferential statistics (t-tests, ANOVA) are applied with coherent interpretation.
Evaluation of methodological choices (MIC vs. ZOI), ethical considerations and discussion of practical limitations demonstrate strong critical reflection.
Arguments are logically structured, linking hypotheses, results and literature throughout, with clear signposting and authentic original data presentation.
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in terminology (e.g., 'cold' vs. 'freezer' extraction) and occasional conflation of temperature categories.
Inferential analyses lack full reporting (missing t-values, degrees of freedom) and adjustments for multiple comparisons (no post-hoc tests, type I error discussion).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (analysis, discussion/evaluation, argument development) fall solidly within the 7â9 band: good, focused analysis; well-supported evaluation; and coherent argumentation with only minor lapses. A score of 9 reflects strong critical thinking without reaching sustained excellence required for the top band.

strengths: Report structure is clear and appropriate: title page, contents, labelled sections, tables and figures are consistently formatted.
Layout considerations (uniform font, double-spacing, correct figure/table formatting) are well applied, with in-text citations, full bibliography, word count and page numbering present.
Photographic documentation and comprehensive table of contents enhance readability and transparency.
weaknesses: Frequent use of first-person phrasing detracts from the formal academic tone.
Minor formatting issues: overly detailed figure captions, missing units in table headers and some TOC entries lacking page numbers.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all criteria for clear and appropriate structure and layout. Despite small formatting inconsistencies and tone issues, the overall quality justifies the maximum 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows standard EE structure with title page, contents, labelled sections, tables, figures and appendix. Sequencing is logical and aids reader navigation.  All core elements are present, satisfying the criteria for a clear and appropriate structure.

Uniform font, double-spacing, correctly formatted tables/figures, in-text citations and full bibliography are provided.  Word count and title appear at the start, pages are numbered.  Minor inconsistencies in figure sizing and table splitting across pages are present but overall layout meets expectations.

Arguments progress logically from hypothesis through data to conclusion, with clear signposting and consistent thread.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g. calling 2 Â°C both âcoldâ and âfreezerâ extraction; conflating 2 Â°C and 7 Â°C in question) prevent a fully seamless narrative, placing the work in the middle of the 4-6 band.

The topicâthe antibacterial efficacy of yerba-mate extractsâ is introduced with clear scientific context, contemporary relevance (antibiotic resistance) and precise organism/variables. Explanations of plant chemistry, bacterial cell-wall differences and the ZOI technique allow a non-specialist to follow the investigation. Minor lapses in phrasing and occasional digressions (e.g. cultural comments) prevent it reaching the absolute highest quality, but overall communication is accurate, engaging and complete.

Literature on yerba-mate chemistry and previous MIC studies is integrated to justify hypotheses and interpret outcomes; citations are frequent and mostly relevant.  However, some statements remain descriptive or rely on generic sources (e.g. Wikipedia) and there is limited critical comparison of differing findings, so application is clear but only partially effective.

Raw data are processed into means and standard deviations; appropriate inferential statistics address each hypothesis and are interpreted correctly.  Analysis remains closely linked to the research question, yet depth is moderateâno confidence intervals or post-hoc tests, and possible confounding factors (disk diffusion variability) are not quantitatively explored.  This fits solid âgoodâ analysis but not the sustained excellence of the top band.

A full experimental procedure, justification of variables, and appropriate statistical tests (t-tests, ANOVA) are provided, indicating a sound methodological choice.  Nevertheless, sample size is small (n=9 discs per treatment), replication rationale is weak, and the rationale for selecting only one bacterial species is acknowledged as a limitation rather than addressed.  The range of secondary sources used to design the method is adequate but not extensive. Hence performance aligns with âgenerally appropriateâ rather than exemplary.

The discussion explains statistical outcomes, links results to literature on compound stability, and identifies practical limitations.  Conclusions are generally well supported, though mechanistic explanations are sometimes speculative and evaluation of uncertainty is brief.  Overall, evaluation is good but not exhaustive.

The research question is explicitly stated, appropriately narrow (temperature and concentration effects on ZOI of B. subtilis) and testable with the chosen method.  Variables, organism and measurement criterion are unambiguously defined, and the student repeatedly links all sections back to this question, showing a strong focus throughout the essay.

Scientific terms (zone of inhibition, MIC, polyphenols, ANOVA, saturation effect) are used accurately and consistently across the report.  Occasional typographical slips do not impede meaning, indicating a high level of conceptual command.

annotations.comment: Ethical considerations are thoroughly addressed, showing awareness of bioethics and data integrity.
The procedural write-up omits justification for replicate number; explain the rationale for sample size to strengthen methodology.
Figure 3âs caption is overly detailed; move full procedural description to the methods section and shorten caption.
Photographic documentation of the ZOI test procedure adds transparency and supports reproducibility.
The title is concise and clearly states the focus of the investigation, effectively communicating the topic.
The research question is clearly stated, with key variables, organism, and measurement defined, ensuring strong focus.
Word count is provided and remains under the 4000-word limit; ensure accuracy by cross-checking it against actual text.
The table of contents is comprehensive and well organized, aiding navigation through the reportâs structure.
The use of first-person phrasing is frequent; adopt a more objective, third-person academic tone to strengthen formal presentation.
Several TOC entries lack page numbers; ensure every section and subsection is referenced with its page number for consistency.
The research question is prominently restated with temperature and concentration variables clearly defined, supporting strong focus.
Clear rationale is provided for choosing Bacillus subtilis, including availability, model status, and safety.
The cultural background paragraph, while interesting, digresses from the scientific focus; consider trimming it or linking it directly to the research rationale.
The distinction between MIC and ZOI methods is acknowledged as a limitation; this demonstrates critical evaluation of methodological choices.
Flow chart lacks step labels and numeric identifiers; adding these would improve clarity of the yerba mate processing sequence.
The link between cell-wall structure and extract efficacy is well explained, demonstrating clear analysis.
Hypothesis 1 is well rooted in existing yerba-mate research, showing coherent application of source material.
The global antibiotic resistance context is cited but could be strengthened with specific data or recent statistics to underscore relevance.
Listing all bioactive compounds is descriptive; focus on those known for antibacterial activity to streamline background application.
Terminology distinguishing antimicrobial from antibacterial activity is accurate and used consistently here.
When listing multiple compounds, consider grouping by chemical class (e.g., terpenoids, acids) to improve conceptual clarity.
Citing a Wikipedia figure undermines source reliability; seek a peer-reviewed or primary source for bacterial cell-wall diagrams.
Hypotheses rely heavily on analogies to green tea; clarify how structural similarities justify extrapolation to yerba-mate.
Null and alternative hypotheses are clearly defined for both variables, showing strong focus and methodological planning.
Table 1 labels lack units in headers and text alignment could be improved to enhance readability.
Describe the exact method for measuring ZOI (e.g., average of two perpendicular diameters) to ensure measurement consistency.
Typographical error âhigher contractionâ should read âhigher concentrationâ; proofread for accuracy in key hypotheses.
H-subscripts in the null and alternative hypotheses should use proper subscript formatting (e.g., Hâ, Hâ).
Including author-titled photos of results enhances authenticity and demonstrates original data collection.
When conducting multiple t-tests, address risk of type I error; consider reporting assumption checks or correcting for multiplicity.
Environmental impact section could be strengthened by referencing specific sustainability standards or certifications.
The results section clearly introduces t-tests and ANOVA, signposting statistical analyses effectively.
Raw data table is dense; consider highlighting mean differences or using color shading to draw attention to key trends.
Consider reporting confidence intervals alongside standard deviations to provide more insight into data variability.
T-test results table omits test statistics (t-value, degrees of freedom); include these to fully report inferential analyses.
Interpretation of non-significant p-values is correct and relates back to hypotheses, demonstrating solid evaluation.
ANOVA results are reported but lack a post-hoc comparison to identify which temperature groups differ significantly.
